# Fleet server rides the standard boot — the cockpit is live-by-default (W2 boot seam, #13015 census)

## Context

The W2 live-wiring census (on the #13015 epic, 2026-07-16) found the seed→live wiring SHIPPED end to end — bridge install unconditional at app boot, fail-closed routing matrices spec-pinned, control round-trip product-wired — with exactly ONE seam missing: `devFleetServer.mjs` starts only via the manual `npm run ai:fleet-server`. Every ordinary boot therefore lands on honestly-labelled JSON seeds with dead controls: the lived "FM is not functional" experience is one missing process, not missing wiring.

## The Problem

The SSOT critical path's clause 03 ("live wiring: the cockpit reads the fleet-services spine live") and clause 04 ("spawn one agent from the UI") are BOTH gated on a server no boot starts. The operator's §04 PoC falsifier — start an agent from the UI instead of a terminal — currently requires knowing to run a second terminal command first, which is the exact failure mode the release gate's "without hand-editing config" clause names.

## The Architectural Reality

- `apps/agentos/app.mjs` already calls `installFleetBridge()` unconditionally (fetch transport → `http://127.0.0.1:8083/fleet`); the browser side needs zero changes.
- `ai/services/fleet/devFleetServer.mjs` is the Option-B dev-server transport host; the Electron shell (Option A) installs an in-process equivalent — this leaf targets the DEV boot path only.
- Candidate shapes (the implementing leaf decides with evidence): (a) the standard dev-server boot (`npm run server-start` / the harness launch path) supervises the fleet server as a sibling process; (b) a compound npm script; (c) the orchestrator/daemon family adopts it as a supervised child. Shape (a) or (c) must handle the port-in-use case honestly (a second checkout must not fight over :8083 — the #15221/#15225 per-process port discipline applies).
- Fail-closed stays intact either way: if the server dies mid-session, the existing degraded/stale routing already covers it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A standard boot path (documented as THE way to run the cockpit) brings up the fleet server without a separate manual command; the cockpit reads live data on first paint of a fresh boot.
- [ ] Port collision handling: a second checkout/boot does not fight over the endpoint (per-process derivation or honest refusal with a named reason — never a silent seed fallback that looks like the cold state).
- [ ] The operable-cold path stays reachable and honest: booting WITHOUT the server (explicitly, or on server death) keeps the fail-closed seed/stale behavior.
- [ ] `learn/` run-the-cockpit documentation names the one boot command.
- [ ] Evidence: a boot-integration witness (spawn the standard boot, assert the cockpit reaches `live` adapter state without manual server start).

## Out of Scope

The operable-cold BANNER (the cockpit stating "fleet server offline" — the census's leaf 2, filed separately) · the Electron in-process bridge (Option A, its own arc) · wake/throttle feed consumption (#15273 + the #15280 arc).

## Related

Epic #13015 (parent) · the W2 census comment (2026-07-16) · #14230 (the download-and-run cornerstone this unlocks FM-side) · #15221/#15225 (port discipline precedent) · D#15249 (the wave's design record).

## Contract Ledger (backfilled per the cycle-1 review — the matrix the command composes under)

| Surface | Source / Authority | Behavior this leaf holds | Fallback (fail-closed) | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **The composed command** `npm run cockpit` | `buildScripts/devCockpit.mjs` (supervisor only — the fleet server stays a Brain entry under `ai/`) | Probes → plans → spawns the fleet transport + the dev server → supervises (signal forwarding; webpack exit tears the session down; fleet exit logs loudly) | A refused plan exits 1 with named reasons — never a silently-broken session | `learn/agentos/RunningTheFleetCockpit.md` (tree-registered) | Composed-boot witness: spawns the REAL launcher, asserts fleet protocol identity on the default endpoint with zero manual starts, SIGTERM teardown returns the endpoint to free |
| **Launch target** | webpack-dev-server `--open-target` | The browser opens `apps/agentos/index.html` — the cockpit surface, not the dev-server root | n/a (open failure leaves both servers serving; the URL is in the docs) | same page | `COCKPIT_OPEN_TARGET` pinned by spec |
| **Producer identity (the reuse gate)** | The fleet wire protocol's deterministic allowlist-rejection envelope (`dispatchFleetRequest`): `POST /fleet {method:'__cockpit_probe__'}` → `ok:false` naming the method, side-effect-free by construction | Three-way probe: `free` → spawn · `fleet` (envelope confirmed) → REUSE with a named line · `incompatible` (any other answer/hang) → REFUSE with the occupant detail | A listener is NEVER presumed to be a fleet server; refusal is the incompatible default | same page (§probes) | Probe witnessed against the REAL transport (real dispatch chain), a non-fleet HTTP envelope (injected dispatch — proves envelope-checking, not HTTP-ness), a bare TCP listener, and a closed port |
| **Consumer endpoint authority** | `apps/agentos/app.mjs` → `installFleetBridge()` pins `http://127.0.0.1:8083/fleet` | ONE endpoint source: the launcher REFUSES non-default `NEO_FLEET_PORT` with a named reason + remedy (a non-default server would never be reached by the browser consumer) | Refusal pre-probe, exit 1; `devFleetServer` standalone keeps honoring the env var | same page (§ports) | Non-default-port refusal witnessed (named reason + the follow-up note) |
| **Endpoint propagation (residual)** | — | Propagating a resolved endpoint through producer AND consumer is a tracked follow-up (this leaf takes the explicit-reject arm the review offered) | The refusal above IS the fail-closed boundary until it lands | noted on the page | — |

— Mnemosyne (@neo-fable, Claude Fable 5). Session 5bbd6fb6-07d8-4f7e-b3ba-cccb66eddcf3.

- 2026-07-16T17:48:32Z @neo-fable referenced in commit `c0ec420` - "feat(agentos): cockpit boot answers cycle-1 — fleet identity probe, endpoint authority, cockpit open-target, run docs (#15283)

The launcher now distinguishes a fleet server from 'some TCP listener': the
probe posts the unlisted __cockpit_probe__ method and only the wire
allowlist's deterministic rejection envelope confirms identity (side-effect-
free by construction). Incompatible occupants and non-default NEO_FLEET_PORT
values are REFUSED with named reasons instead of composing silently-broken
sessions. The dev server opens apps/agentos/index.html directly, the
composed-boot integration witness observes a real fresh boot reaching fleet
protocol identity with zero manual server starts, and the run-the-cockpit
learn page ships tree-registered."
- 2026-07-16T18:30:11Z @tobiu referenced in commit `6ee9742` - "feat(build): npm run cockpit — the one-command live-by-default FM boot (#15283) (#15288)

* feat(build): npm run cockpit — the one-command live-by-default FM boot (#15283)

The W2 boot seam: the seed→live wiring shipped end-to-end, but devFleetServer only
started via a separate manual command — every ordinary boot landed on sample seeds
with dead controls. buildScripts/devCockpit.mjs supervises both processes:

- probes the fleet port FIRST (shared-machine honesty: a busy :8083 means another
  checkout or a prior run owns the transport — reuse with a named log line, never a
  silent second server; NEO_FLEET_PORT passes through)
- spawns devFleetServer + npm run server-start (byte-untouched) with stdio inherit
- signal forwarding both ways; webpack exit tears the session down; a fleet-server
  exit logs the loss loudly while the cockpit degrades to its honest offline states
- pure decision seam (planCockpitBoot/probePort) exported for the witnesses: 3 specs
  green (spawn-vs-reuse matrix + probe against a real ephemeral listener)

server-start unchanged; ai/ untouched — the launcher only supervises.
